title: What to know about Calendula officinalis before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Calendula officinalis, commonly known as pot marigold, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and infections, as well as digestive issues and menstrual disorders. Its main health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, promoting tissue repair and wound healing. The herb's therapeutic properties are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and immunomodulatory effects. Calendula officinalis contains bioactive compounds such as flavonoids, saponins, and carotenoids. People typically prepare calendula as a topical cream or ointment for skin applications.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the health benefits of Zingiber officinale include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate fever, as evidenced by its ability to decrease carrageenan-induced paw swelling and yeast-induced fever in rats. Additionally, the extract has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria. Furthermore, the study suggests that Zingiber officinale may also have antidiabetic properties, as it produced blood glucose lowering in rabbits.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Calendula officinalis are treating burns by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, largely attributed to triterpene saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. This plant also relieves eczema symptoms, such as redness and itching, through its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, primarily attributed to triterpene saponins and flavonoids. Additionally, Calendula officinalis accelerates wound healing, including skin ulcers and infections, by promoting tissue repair and preventing infection, thanks to its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, mainly due to triterpene saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. Calendula officinalis alleviates skin irritation and wounds, including bruises, by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair, attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic activities, largely due to triterpene saponins and flavonoids. Furthermore, it treats acne and insect bites by reducing inflammation and preventing infection, primarily attributed to its antibacterial and antifungal properties, mainly due to phenolic acids and flavonoids.

uses_list.description: It is used for burns due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce pain, prevent infection, and promote wound healing.
The dried flowers of the plant are used to treat eczema as they possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and itching.
Its topical application is used to aid wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue strength, and reducing the formation of scar tissue.
The plant is used to treat skin irritation due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling.
It is used to treat wounds, such as cuts and abrasions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent infection and promote wound healing.
The plant is used to treat bruises due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce pain, swelling, and discoloration.
It is used to treat acne due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of acne symptoms, such as redness and inflammation.
The plant is used to treat insect bites due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness, itching, and swelling.
It is used to treat skin ulcers due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent infection and promote wound healing.
The plant is used to treat skin infections due to its antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the spread of infection and promote wound healing.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Calendula officinalis is used to treat burns by promoting tissue repair and reducing pain. It is also applied to eczema to soothe itchy skin and reduce inflammation. Calendula officinalis has been shown to accelerate wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis and improving tissue strength. Additionally, it is used to treat skin irritation caused by minor cuts and scrapes, acne by reducing inflammation and preventing bacterial growth, wounds by promoting tissue regeneration and reducing scarring, and diaper rash by soothing irritated skin. Furthermore, Calendula officinalis is used to treat inflammatory conditions such as psoriasis by reducing inflammation and promoting skin cell turnover, and herpes simplex by reducing the severity and duration of outbreaks.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Calendula officinalis is used to treat burns, its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties making it an effective treatment for first and second-degree burns, while also being used to treat eczema, a chronic skin condition characterized by inflammation and skin barrier dysfunction. Calendula officinalis is also valued for its role in wound healing, promoting tissue repair and granulation, as well as treating wounds, which benefit from its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. This medicinal plant is also used to treat bruises, reducing inflammation and promoting the resolution of blood extravasation, in addition to its applications in treating skin infections, such as bacterial and fungal infections. Furthermore, Calendula officinalis is employed to alleviate skin irritation, including redness and itching, and to treat insect bites, which benefit from its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Additionally, it has been used to help reduce fever and to treat acne, a common skin condition characterized by inflammation and bacterial overgrowth.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Calendula officinalis is used to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis and tissue repair, while also treating eczema with its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Topical application of Calendula officinalis is effective in soothing burns and reducing pain, as well as acne by reducing inflammation and preventing clogged pores. Additionally, it is used to alleviate skin irritation caused by allergic reactions or environmental factors, and to treat vaginal infections such as bacterial vaginosis due to its antimicrobial properties. Calendula officinalis is also known to reduce the severity of bruises by improving circulation and promoting tissue repair, and to treat skin infections by preventing bacterial growth. Furthermore, it is used to alleviate diarrhea by reducing inflammation in the gut and promoting the healing of the intestinal lining, and to treat inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Calendula officinalis is used to treat various skin conditions. Calendula officinalis is widely used for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties in the treatment of burns, which accelerates the healing process and reduces the risk of infection. The plant is also effective in treating eczema, an inflammatory skin condition characterized by dry, itchy, and scaly skin. Additionally, Calendula officinalis is employed to enhance wound healing, skin ulcers, and wounds by promoting tissue regeneration and collagen synthesis. Furthermore, it is used to treat wounds, bruises, acne, fungal infections, herpes, and skin irritation due to its antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory activities.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Calendula officinalis is used to treat various skin conditions. Calendula officinalis is often employed to alleviate the discomfort associated with burns, promoting faster healing and reducing scarring. It is also utilized to manage eczema, providing relief from inflammation and itching. Additionally, the plant is used to address skin irritation, including minor irritations caused by insect bites. Calendula officinalis has been found to aid in the healing of bruises, as well as wounds, minor cuts, and acne, while also reducing the appearance of skin irritations.
benefits_description: Calendula officinalis reduces inflammation by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating redness and swelling. The plant heals skin wounds by accelerating the process of epithelialization and promoting the formation of granulation tissue, which eventually leads to the closure of wounds. Soothing digestive issues is facilitated by the anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties of Calendula officinalis, which reduce inflammation and spasms in the gastrointestinal tract. Relieving menstrual cramps is attributed to the flavonoids present in the plant, which have a spasmolytic effect on smooth muscle tissue. Calendula officinalis prevents skin infections by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, including the inhibition of the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses, accelerates wound healing by promoting the synthesis of collagen and enhancing the production of growth factors, decreases scarring by reducing the deposition of collagen, treats oral lesions by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi, fights viral infections by activating immune cells, and reduces acne outbreaks by reducing sebum production and inhibiting the growth of Propionibacterium acnes.

benefits_list.description: This benefit is attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and other compounds in the plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate associated pain and swelling.

The plant's high concentration of triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids, as well as its ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and improve blood flow, contribute to its wound-healing properties, promoting the formation of new tissue and the restoration of skin integrity.

The plant's flavonoids, terpenoids, and other compounds have been found to have a protective effect on the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation, improving gut barrier function, and alleviating symptoms of digestive disorders such as irritable bowel syndrome.

The plant's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, particularly its flavonoids and terpenoids, help to reduce cramping, relax smooth muscle, and alleviate other symptoms associated with menstrual cramps, such as anxiety and mood disturbances.

The plant's antimicrobial properties, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses, help to prevent skin infections, promote a healthy skin microbiome, and reduce the risk of wound complications.

The plant's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis, improve blood flow, and promote tissue repair, as well as its antimicrobial properties, contribute to its wound-healing properties, accelerating the process of tissue repair and restoring skin integrity.

The plant's ability to stimulate collagen synthesis and promote tissue repair, as well as its anti-inflammatory properties, help to reduce the formation of scar tissue, minimizing the appearance of scars and promoting smoother skin texture.

The plant's antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, help to treat oral lesions, such as oral thrush, reducing inflammation, promoting tissue repair, and alleviating associated symptoms.

The plant's flavonoids, terpenoids, and other compounds have been found to have antiviral properties, helping to inhibit the replication of viruses, reduce the severity of viral infections, and alleviate associated symptoms, such as fever and fatigue.

The plant's anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, particularly its ability to reduce the growth of P. acnes bacteria, help to reduce acne outbreaks, alleviate associated symptoms, such as redness and inflammation, and promote a healthy skin microbiome.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Calendula officinalis improves blood flow by relaxing blood vessels and reducing blood pressure, making it easier for blood to circulate throughout the body, and enhances circulation by promoting the formation of new blood vessels, which in turn boosts red blood cell production and increases oxygen delivery to tissues. The anti-inflammatory properties of Calendula officinalis soothe inflamed veins and prevent blood clots from forming, supporting heart health by maintaining healthy blood vessels and preventing conditions like atherosclerosis. Additionally, Calendula officinalis prevents blood clots by inhibiting platelet aggregation, which enhances wound healing by promoting the formation of new tissue and reducing inflammation, and also reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease by promoting healthy blood vessel function. Calendula officinalis has antioxidant properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, and its antimicrobial properties prevent infections that can compromise blood vessel health. By promoting healthy blood vessel function and preventing cardiovascular disease, Calendula officinalis supports heart health, making it an effective herbal remedy for circulatory system health.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Calendula officinalis soothes digestive issues by reducing inflammation with its anti-inflammatory properties, while its antimicrobial properties fight off bacteria in the digestive tract. This plant heals intestinal wounds with its antioxidant properties, preventing the formation of ulcers. Calendula officinalis tones gut muscles, enhancing gut motility through its direct action on the smooth muscle layer. The plant relaxes the smooth sphincter, allowing for the free flow of digestive content, and stimulates digestive secretion with its trophic properties. By protecting the intestinal mucosa with its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, Calendula officinalis promotes a healthy digestive system.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Calendula officinalis regulates hormone production by its antiproliferative activity, inhibiting the growth of cancerous cells, which can lead to unbalanced hormone levels. Calendula officinalis stimulates thyroid function due to its antioxidant properties, scavenging free radicals that damage thyroid cells, thereby promoting thyroid health. Calendula officinalis reduces inflammation in the endocrine system through its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, preventing infections that can disrupt hormone balance. Calendula officinalis balances adrenal function by its adaptogenic properties, mitigating stress-induced cortisol surges, and reducing the risk of adrenal fatigue. Calendula officinalis supports pancreas health through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, protecting pancreatic cells from oxidative damage and inflammation, and promoting insulin production.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Calendula officinalis heals wounds due to its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause infections. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation, thereby promoting a conducive environment for the wound-healing process. Calendula officinalis protects skin from damage caused by free radicals, thanks to its antioxidant properties, which neutralize oxidative stress and prevent cell damage. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also enable calendula to repair tissues by reducing oxidative stress and preventing infection. Calendula officinalis soothes irritations and fades scars by promoting regeneration of skin cells, thereby restoring skin health and appearance, and cleansing the skin by removing dead skin cells, while its antioxidant properties safeguard skin from further damage.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Calendula officinalis stimulates lymph flow by promoting the contraction and relaxation of lymphatic vessels, thanks to its vasodilatory properties. This plant boosts lymph function by enhancing the activity of lymphocytes, which are white blood cells responsible for immune response, due to its immunomodulatory effects. Calendula officinalis reduces lymph swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, such as histamine and bradykinin, which cause edema, thanks to its anti-inflammatory and antihistaminic properties. Additionally, calendula activates lymph cells, like macrophages and dendritic cells, which are crucial for the recognition and elimination of pathogens, due to its antimicrobial and antiviral properties. By combining these actions, calendula increases lymph drainage, supports lymph health, enhances lymph detox, protects lymph tissue, promotes lymph balance, and regulates the lymph system, ultimately contributing to the maintenance of immune homeostasis and the prevention of lymph-related disorders.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Calendula officinalis reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. The plant relieves pain by containing triterpenoid saponins, which have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects. Additionally, calendula soothes irritation due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to protect the skin from infection and promote healing. Furthermore, the plant heals wounds by stimulating collagen synthesis and enhancing wound contraction, while promoting tissue repair through the activation of fibroblasts. Calendula fights infection by exhibiting antimicrobial activity against a range of pathogens, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which accelerates recovery by reducing the risk of infection and promoting the healing process, ultimately enhancing mobility and reducing swelling, and improving flexibility through the reduction of pain and inflammation.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Calendula officinalis reduces anxiety by exerting its adaptogenic properties, which help balance the body's response to stress. Stimulated by the plant's flavonoids and triterpenoids, focus is enhanced due to its nootropic effects, allowing the mind to stay alert and concentrated. Calm nerves and relax tension are achieved through the plant's sedative and anxiolytic properties, which counteract the negative effects of stress hormones. Improved mood is a result of the plant's serotonin-boosting properties, which regulate emotional responses and promote feelings of well-being. By soothing irritability, boosting energy, and reducing stress, Calendula officinalis ultimately promotes relaxation and enhances concentration.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Calendula officinalis soothes menstrual cramps by exerting anti-inflammatory properties, thereby reducing pain and discomfort. Its antimicrobial properties fight fungal infections, while also preventing sepsis by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. Calendula's antioxidant activity stabilizes hormones, supporting ovulation and promoting healthy reproductive function. Additionally, its antifungal properties treat yeast infections by disrupting fungal cell walls and preventing their growth. The plant's ability to heal wounds faster is partly due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which also promote skin health.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Calendula officinalis soothe bronchitis by its anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation and relieving congestion, which enables it to alleviate coughs and clear mucus from the respiratory system, also preventing infections by its antimicrobial properties, and even treat allergies by its antioxidant activity, while its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also relieve asthma and strengthen lungs, and its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties also combat tuberculosis by inhibiting the growth of the tuberculosis bacteria.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Calendula officinalis prevents bladder infections due to its antimicrobial properties that inhibit the growth of pathogenic bacteria, such as E. coli, responsible for these infections. Calendula officinalis treats urinary tract inflammation by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Calendula officinalis heals kidney damage through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and protect kidney cells from oxidative stress. Calendula officinalis reduces urinary tract symptoms by exhibiting antispasmodic properties, which relax the smooth muscles and relieve spasms. Calendula officinalis prevents kidney stones by dissolving calcium oxalate crystals, the primary component of kidney stones, due to its chelating properties.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Calendula officinalis are multifaceted, exhibiting antimicrobial properties due to its ability to inhibit the growth of microorganisms, while also displaying antioxidant properties through the scavenging of free radicals and the protection of cells from oxidative damage. Its anti-inflammatory properties play a crucial role in soothing and calming irritated tissues, reducing redness and swelling. The plant's antiseptic properties are attributed to the presence of triterpene acids and flavonoids, which contribute to its antiseptic and antipyretic effects, helping to reduce fever and alleviate discomfort. Furthermore, Calendula officinalis has been shown to possess antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it an effective agent against a wide range of pathogens. Additionally, the bioactive compounds present in the plant, including flavonoids and triterpene acids, are responsible for its wound healing, skin soothing, and anti-itching properties, promoting tissue repair and regeneration.

properties_list.description: Calendula officinalis has been found to exhibit antimicrobial activity, inhibiting the growth of various microorganisms such as bacteria and fungi, which can contribute to wound infections and other health issues.

The plant contains flavonoids and other compounds that have antioxidant properties, helping to neutralize free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage, which can contribute to aging and various diseases.

Calendula officinalis has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, reducing inflammation and swelling in the body, which can help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases.
The plant has been used as an antiseptic to prevent the growth of microorganisms and to promote wound healing by reducing the risk of infection.
Calendula officinalis has been traditionally used to treat fever and to reduce body temperature, although its effectiveness for this purpose has not been extensively scientifically studied.
The plant has been found to exhibit antibacterial activity, inhibiting the growth of various bacteria that can cause infections and other health issues.
Calendula officinalis has been found to exhibit antifungal activity, inhibiting the growth of various fungi that can cause infections and other health issues.
The plant has been traditionally used to promote wound healing by reducing inflammation, preventing infection, and promoting tissue repair.
Calendula officinalis has been used to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation, and promoting healing.
The plant has been traditionally used to relieve itching and other skin irritations, although its effectiveness for this purpose has not been extensively scientifically studied.

constituents.description: A type of polyphenolic compound in Calendula officinalis with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
Carotenoids have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and photoprotective properties.
Saponins are a type of bioactive compound in Calendula officinalis characterized by their surface-active and foaming properties.
Phenolic acids from Calendula officinalis possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
A constituent of Calendula officinalis, Triterpenoids are known for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.
Calendula officinalis Glycosides have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, with the ability to stimulate wound healing and exhibit antioxidant activity.
Quercetin is a flavonoid antioxidant with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties

Calendula officinalis contains triterpenoid saponins and not alkaloids. However, a few studies have reported the presence of a small amount of alkaloids in the plant but their properties are not well documented.
Kaempferol is a flavonoid constituent of Calendula officinalis with anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
Phytosterols are constituents of Calendula officinalis with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Calendula officinalis are flavonoids, including quercetin, kaempferol, and luteolin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to its wound-healing and antiseptic activities. Carotenoids, such as beta-carotene and lutein, possess antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from oxidative damage and promoting tissue repair. Saponins, like oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, display immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory effects, further supporting the plant's use in wound care. Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, adding to the plant's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Triterpenoids, including calanolides and calendolides, have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to the plant's wound-healing and antiseptic activities.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Calendula officinalis for medicinal purposes are flowers and leaves. The flowers of Calendula officinalis contain flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, and triterpene saponins, which are responsible for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for wound healing and skin conditions. The leaves of Calendula officinalis are rich in flavonoids like rutin and kaempferol, as well as volatile oils, particularly borneol and camphor, which contribute to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, useful for treating skin irritations and infections. The bioactive compounds in the flowers and leaves of Calendula officinalis also exhibit antimicrobial and antihistamine properties, providing relief from allergic reactions and skin disorders. These medicinal properties make Calendula officinalis a popular herbal remedy for various skin conditions and injuries.

parts_list.description: The flowers of this plant are rich in calendulin, triterpene saponins, and flavonoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, providing primary health benefits such as wound healing, skin protection, and reduced inflammation.

The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, providing primary health benefits such as reduced inflammation, immune system support, and protection against oxidative stress.

preparations.description: Cream made from this plant is used for wound healing, skin irritation, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
preparations.overview: Calendula officinalis cream is a topical preparation commonly used to treat various skin conditions, including eczema, burns, wounds, acne, cuts, skin irritation, insect bites, rashes, sunburn, and bruises. It's widely used due to its effectiveness in soothing and healing skin issues. One of its most common effects is promoting skin regeneration and reducing inflammation. The cream is made from the flowers and leaves of the Calendula officinalis plant, which are rich in triterpenoid saponins and carotenoids that provide its medicinal properties. While it can be made at home, it requires some knowledge and skills to prepare correctly, falling into the moderately difficult category of herbal preparations.
recipe: Combine 1 cup of dried Calendula officinalis flowers and leaves with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant container to create a floral infusion.
Steep the mixture for 20-30 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Combine the infused liquid with 1/2 cup of beeswax, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Heat the mixture, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax has melted and the ingredients have combined, then remove from heat.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken, then stir in 10 drops of vitamin E oil and 5 drops of lavender essential oil to create a soothing and moisturizing herbal cream.

preparations.description: Salve made from this plant is topically applied to treat wound infections, inflammation, and skin irritation, as well as to promote wound healing, soothe burns, and protect damaged skin.
preparations.overview: Calendula officinalis salve is widely used to treat a variety of skin issues including eczema, burns, wounds, skin irritations, bruises, cuts, rashes, acne, insect bites, and minor burns due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It's a very common preparation found in many households and first aid kits. The effect of calendula salve is often observed as reduced inflammation, faster healing of wounds, and improvement of skin health. The preparation is typically made from the flowers and leaves of the Calendula officinalis plant, which are rich in triterpenoids and carotenoids that provide its therapeutic benefits. Making calendula salve is moderately difficult and requires some knowledge of herbalism and preparation techniques, but it can be a rewarding process to create a natural remedy for common skin issues.
recipe: Gather 1 cup of fresh Calendula officinalis flowers and leaves, and dry them completely in a warm, dark place.
Combine 1/4 cup of dried Calendula flowers and leaves with 2 cups of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily, to allow the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids, and add 2 tablespoons of beeswax.

Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax is fully melted and the salve has reached a temperature of 120°F to 140°F, then pour it into small tin containers and allow it to cool and solidify.

preparations.description: Ointment made from this plant is used to treat minor skin irritations, wounds, and burns, and to promote wound healing and tissue repair.
preparations.overview: Calendula officinalis ointment is a herbal preparation used to treat various skin conditions, including burns, eczema, wounds, bruises, cuts, acne, psoriasis, skin irritation, rashes, and insect bites. It is a common preparation used by many individuals due to its efficacy in soothing and healing the skin. One of the common effects of this ointment is its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The preparation is typically made from the flowers and leaves of the Calendula officinalis plant, which are rich in antioxidants and other bioactive compounds. However, producing this ointment can be challenging due to the need for precise extraction and processing of the plant material to ensure the desired therapeutic properties are retained.
recipe: Harvest fresh Calendula officinalis flowers and leaves in their peak potency.
Dry the harvested flowers and leaves using a dehydrator or by air-drying them for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried flowers and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the powdered flowers and leaves with 8 tablespoons of beeswax and 8 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 180°F, then remove from heat and let it cool before straining and storing the ointment in a cool, dark place.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used topically and internally to treat wounds, skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis, and to aid in healing of ulcers and mucous membranes, and as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent.
preparations.overview: Calendula officinalis tincture is a herbal preparation commonly used to treat a variety of skin issues, including eczema, burns, skin irritation, insect bites, bruises, wounds, wound healing, minor cuts, and acne. It's also used to treat diaper rash. Calendula officinalis tincture is a widely used and recognized remedy in herbal medicine. Its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties are the common effects observed with its use. The preparation is typically made from the flowers and leaves of the Calendula officinalis plant. While it's possible to make the tincture at home, the process can be moderately difficult, requiring some knowledge of herbal extraction techniques.
recipe: Gather fresh Calendula officinalis flowers and leaves, and clean them thoroughly.
Combine 100 grams of the cleaned Calendula flowers and leaves in a clean glass jar.
Add 500 milliliters of a 40% ethanol solution to the jar, ensuring that all plant material is completely covered.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the jar every few days to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

preparations.description: Gel made from this plant is used topically to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and protect skin from infection in minor cuts, scrapes, and burns, and as an anti-irritant for skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
preparations.overview: Calendula officinalis gel is used to treat burns, skin irritations, wounds, eczema, cuts, minor cuts, acne, insect bites, rashes, and cutaneous ulcers. This preparation is widely used due to its effectiveness and availability. A common effect of Calendula officinalis gel is its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations. It is typically made from the flowers and leaves of the Calendula officinalis plant, which are rich in triterpenoids and flavonoids that possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The process of making Calendula officinalis gel is moderately difficult, requiring the extraction and purification of these compounds to create a potent and effective topical solution.
recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh Calendula officinalis flowers and leaves, and clean them thoroughly.
Combine the clean flowers and leaves with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes, then let it steep for an additional 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the strained liquid with 2 tablespoons of a gelling agent like agar or carrageenan, and heat until the gel forms.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Calendula officinalis for medicinal purposes are creams, which are often applied topically to soothe and protect skin irritations such as wounds, eczema, and minor burns. Calendula salve is commonly used to treat skin conditions like acne, dermatitis, and ulcers, by promoting wound healing and reducing inflammation. Ointments, on the other hand, are typically employed to treat dry skin conditions like chapped lips and cracked hands, due to their emollient properties. Tinctures, often administered orally, are used to treat digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome and gastritis, where Calendula's anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe the gut. Calendula gel is also used topically to reduce inflammation and promote healing in conditions such as sprains, strains, and minor injuries.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Contact dermatitis, urticaria, and angioedema occur due to cross-reactivity with other plants in the Compositae family.
Contact with Calendula officinalis causes skin redness, inflammation, and vesiculation.
Skin irritation occurs due to allergenic compounds in Calendula officinalis.
Calendula officinalis extracts cause increased skin sensitivity to sunlight, leading to burns or blisters.
Consuming high amounts of Calendula officinalis extracts can cause gastrointestinal irritation and stomach upset.
Calendula officinalis may increase or decrease the effects of anticoagulant, anticonvulsant, and blood thinning medications.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Calendula officinalis are allergic reactions occur when the skin comes into contact with the plant's sap, triggering symptoms such as hives, itching, and difficulty breathing. Skin irritation develops rapidly, causing redness, swelling, and pain, especially if the plant's essential oils are not properly diluted. Contact dermatitis happens when the skin's immune system reacts to the plant's allergens, leading to increased sensitivity and inflammation. Photosensitivity reactions occur when the plant's compounds interact with sunlight, causing burns, blisters, and discoloration of the skin. Digestive issues arise in individuals who ingest large quantities of Calendula officinalis, potentially leading to nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain, while interactions with medications occur, particularly with blood thinners and diabetes medications.

precautions.precaution_description: Pregnant women, breastfeeding mothers, and individuals with allergies or autoimmune disorders should consult a healthcare professional before using Calendula officinalis.
Some individuals may experience allergic reactions, such as contact dermatitis, due to calendula oil or extracts.
Calendula officinalis extracts may stimulate uterine contractions; use should be avoided by pregnant women.
Calendula officinalis may cause uterine contractions and stimulate milk production, posing a risk to breastfeeding mothers.
Adjust dosage or discontinue Calendula officinalis if blood sugar levels change.
Patients with hypertension should monitor blood pressure due to Calendula officinalis' potential vasoconstrictive effects.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Calendula officinalis medicinally are consulting a healthcare professional to determine the proper dosage and ensure safe use, especially when assessing for allergy since some individuals may experience skin irritation or allergic reactions, and it is also crucial to avoid its use during pregnancy due to potential hormonal effects, furthermore, breastfeeding mothers should also avoid using Calendula officinalis as it may affect milk production or infant gut health, in addition, patients with diabetes should monitor their blood sugar levels closely as Calendula officinalis may lower blood sugar levels, and individuals with hypertension should closely monitor their blood pressure as it may cause blood pressure fluctuations.
identification: Calendula officinalis is an annual or perennial herbaceous plant with a height of 20-80 cm, branching stems, and leaves that are simple, alternate, and lanceolate with a pointed apex and serrated margin. The plant's flowers are daisy-like, measuring 3-6 cm in diameter, with 8-15 yellow or orange petals and 100-200 disc florets. Calendula officinalis is native to the Mediterranean region, but it has been naturalized in many parts of the world, including Europe, Asia, Africa, and North America. It prefers well-drained soil and full sun to partial shade, and is often found growing in fields, roadsides, and other disturbed areas. The plant's geographical range has been expanded through human activity, and it is now considered an invasive species in some regions.
cultivation: Calendula officinalis is a hardy annual that thrives in well-draining soil and full sun, requiring temperatures between 15°C and 30°C (59°F and 86°F) to grow. It prefers a slightly acidic to neutral soil pH (6.0-7.0) and can tolerate poor soil conditions, making it suitable for poor soil or areas with low water tables. To promote healthy growth, it's essential to sow seeds directly into the ground in early spring or late summer, and provide adequate moisture, especially during flowering, which can be encouraged by deadheading spent blooms. Regular watering, fertilization, and mulching can also help to prevent pests and diseases, such as leaf spot and root rot, which can be mitigated by using crop rotation and avoiding overwatering. By following these cultivation guidelines, Calendula officinalis can be successfully grown in a variety of environments, producing an abundance of flowers for medicinal and ornamental use.
history: Calendula officinalis is a plant that has been used historically as a medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. Its flowers have been employed to treat skin conditions such as wounds, burns, eczema, and acne due to their ability to promote tissue repair and reduce inflammation. In traditional medicine, Calendula was used to treat gastrointestinal issues like ulcers and diarrhea, as well as to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems like bronchitis and pneumonia. The plant's antiseptic properties made it a popular treatment for infections, including those affecting the eyes and ears. Calendula's uses were extensively documented in ancient Greek, Roman, and Ayurvedic medicine, and it remains a widely used herbal remedy today.
